rulings.Case Title: ALI KIA SHABAHANGI et al VS. NADER SHABAHANGI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiffs To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Agesong, Inc To Special Interrogatories, Set 1
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday May 7, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS ALI SHABAHANGI, AMIR KIA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Agesong, Inc To Special Interrogatories, Set 1. Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court having considered Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses by Defendant Agesong, Inc. to special interrogatories set 1 and also having considered defendant Agesong, Inc's opposition to that motion, the court hereby grants the motion. The procedural requirements have been met to hear the motion. This is a complex case involving multiple transactions and properties encompassing millions of dollars. The special interrogatories are helpful in narrowing the issues and discovering relevant information. The interrogatories were not propounded in bad faith. However, they were sent late in the case close to the end of discovery cut off. If the trial is still set to begin on June 1, 2015 it is recommended that further efforts to continue the trial be made so that the discovery can be obtained, submitted, evaluated and used at trial. The further responses are due no later than May 21, 2015. The request for sanctions is denied because it is procedurally defective and also because there was a good faith reason to object to the extensive discovery which was sent in the final stages of the case, close to the discovery cut off before trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS T. MANGANO VS. THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute. Denied without prejudice to being refiled on or after November 9, 2015 if a hearing on a writ has not been scheduled by that date. There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits, settlement discussions between the parties have led to some delay in prosecution of the case, and plaintiff recently retained counsel who represents that he intends to calendar a hearing on a writ of mandate in the near future. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT KUO VS. PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first through sixth and eighth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. The first amended complaint ("FAC") sufficiently alleges that defendants made misrepresentations in order to induce plaintiff to accept an offer of employment. (FAC, pars. 1, 3, 13, 15-16, 27-29, 31.) The FAC adequately alleges that the "requisite approval" and the execution of a "formal, written agreement," were conditions precedent to performance. The FAC also adequately alleges that defendants prevented the happening of the condition precedent. (FAC, par. 21.) It is a factual question beyond the scope of demurrer whether this would excuse the condition. (Rains v. Arnett (1961) 189 Cal.App.2d 337, 347.) The promissory estoppel claim is adequately pled, and such a claim made be alleged in the alternative to the breach of contract cause of action. (Fleet v. Bank of America N.A. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1413.) The FAC adequately pleads a section 17200 claim that, but for Defendants' unfair and/or fraudulent conduct, plaintiff would have been entitled to accelerated vesting of his shares at the time of the merger. These allegations are sufficient to establish standing. The wrongful termination cause of action fails as a matter of law. The Labor Code statutes cited in paragraph 46 of the FAC require that plaintiff have a vested right to the compensation. The FAC alleges that plaintiff only had an expectation that he would receive this compensation. Because plaintiff cannot plead he had a vested right to the stock at the time of termination, he has not pled facts establishing a violation of the Labor Code. The demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave because plaintiff had previously been given the opportunity to amend to state a cause of action, and was unable to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BAKER ET AL VS. NINA MACKENZIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday May 7, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DAVID BAKER'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court having considered Plaintiff's Motion for Protective Order and Defendant's opposition thereto, the court hereby denies the motion. The procedural requirements have been met to hear the motion. The discovery requests seek information which may lead to admissible evidence with respect to the issues in these consolidated cases. Discovery my proceed during the pendency of the hearing on the Demurrer. Plaintiff has failed to prove with sufficiency that the Demurrer will be granted. An order preventing the discovery sought would delay discovery efforts. The discovery sought is not only directed to the issues of the three probate claims which are the subject of the Demurrer. That said, the parties are ordered to meet and confer on an appropriate confidentiality agreement which will keep the discovery private and to be used only for purposes of this litigation. A discovery referee is also recommend, to be paid for jointly by the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW KUMIN VS. STEPHEN SOMMERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement [C.C.P. Â§664.6]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW KUMIN Motion To Enforce Settlement [C.C.P. Sec. 664.6]. Granted. The parties entered into a valid agreement to divide assets of their former partnership through November 30, 2012. Defendant shall: (1) provide plaintiff with an accounting for all sums owed to plaintiff on the Menbreno matter and pay all sums owed to plaintiff on the Menbreno matter within 15 days of the Court's order; (2) provide plaintiff with an accounting for all sums owed to plaintiff in the Res Care matter and pay all sums owed to plaintiff on the Res Care matter within 15 days of defendant's receipt of the Res Care funds; and (3) pay plaintiff reasonable attorney's fees and costs within 15 days of receipt of plaintiff's accounting of such fees and costs as provided for in the settlement agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LLOYD UYEHARA ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF ALL OTHERS SIMI VS. GOLFSMITH USA LLC ***TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 397)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT GOLFSMITH USA LLC Motion To Transfer Venue (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 167; 397). The matter is taken Off calendar at the request of the moving Party. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA M. ROPP VS. LABOR COMMISSIONER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 9, Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued. The matter is taken Off calendar based on the representations from counsel for defendants that there are no remaining issues in dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(HK/302)

rulings.Case Title: AVOCA TECHNOLOGIES INC. VS. ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 7, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF AVOCA TECHNOLOGIES INC. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)
